data.description:
## Test remediation: defensive autouse guard against global enforcement

Assessment: flipping `require_authenticated` in this project's `rebar.toml` does NOT break
the suite (every test store is an isolated tmp `REBAR_ROOT`; config discovery never reaches
the real `rebar.toml`; `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is stubbed). But a global env
`REBAR_IDENTITY_REQUIRE_AUTHENTICATED=1` DOES break it broadly (empirically: every
create/mutate of a non-exempt type fails with the "cannot be signed" CommandError).

- Add an autouse fixture to the repo-root `tests/conftest.py` that forces the gate OFF for the suite (mirroring the existing `_gate_source_local_by_default` / `_compaction_horizon_zero_by_default` idiom, `tests/conftest.py:200-232`): `monkeypatch.setenv("REBAR_IDENTITY_REQUIRE_AUTHENTICATED", "0")`.
- Confirm it does NOT disturb the dedicated identity tests (`tests/unit/test_identity_*`), which build their own subprocess env with the flag ON (their explicit value wins).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `tests/conftest.py` has an autouse fixture forcing `REBAR_IDENTITY_REQUIRE_AUTHENTICATED=0` for the suite, documented as a guard against a stray global default.
- [ ] The full suite is green both with and without a stray global `REBAR_IDENTITY_REQUIRE_AUTHENTICATED=1` in the environment.
- [ ] The dedicated identity enforcement tests still pass unchanged.
data.title: Test remediation: autouse conftest guard against global enforcement env
